extra.reasoning:
chest_pain appears in only one rule, r9 (priority 100): IF (fever and palpitations and slurred_speech and pallor and vision_loss and rash and back_pain and chest_pain) THEN acuity := self_care. The monotonicity property requires that flipping chest_pain false->true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity.

Counterexample baseline: fever=palpitations=slurred_speech=pallor=vision_loss=rash=back_pain=true, hemoptysis=false, chest_pain=FALSE. Here r9 does not fire (chest_pain missing). Among r1-r8, the priority-3 rules r5 (pallor->emergent), r7 (rash->emergent), and r8 (palpitations->emergent) match, beating the priority-2 rules. Result = emergent (enum value 3).

Now set chest_pain=TRUE with everything else identical (the witness_assignment given). All 8 conjuncts of r9 are now satisfied, so r9 fires with priority 100, dominating all other rules. Result = self_care (enum value 0).

Setting chest_pain true drops acuity from emergent (3) to self_care (0), a strict decrease. This violates the monotonicity property "setting chest_pain true never lowers acuity." The witness_assignment is the post-flip input (chest_pain=true); its pre-flip counterpart differs only in chest_pain=false and yields emergent.
